title.es: Seguro Medico para Propietarios Unicos 2026 | CoveredUSA

description.en: Sole proprietors: ACA marketplace, HDHP with HSA, ICHRA rules, Form 7206 deduction. 2026 options for Schedule C filers and 1099 contractors.
description.es: Propietarios unicos: mercado ACA, HDHP con HSA, reglas ICHRA, deduccion Formulario 7206. Opciones 2026 para Schedule C y contratistas 1099.

h1.en: Health Insurance for Sole Proprietors in 2026
h1.es: Seguro Medico para Propietarios Unicos en 2026

subhero.en: Sole proprietors and Schedule C filers who go without an employer plan have three real paths: ACA marketplace subsidies, an HSA-paired HDHP for the triple tax play, or a spouse plan. The Form 7206 deduction makes 100% of premiums deductible above the line. The 2026 subsidy cliff is back at 400% FPL, which changes the math above $63,840 for single filers.
subhero.es: Los propietarios unicos y Schedule C sin plan de empleador tienen tres caminos reales: subsidios del mercado ACA, un HDHP con HSA para la ventaja triple fiscal, o el plan del conyuge. La deduccion del Formulario 7206 hace el 100% de las primas deducibles. El tope del subsidio de 2026 regreso al 400% FPL, lo que cambia los calculos por encima de $63,840 para solteros.

quickAnswer.en: Sole proprietors, Schedule C filers, and other self-employed individuals without employees typically choose between (1) an ACA Marketplace plan with Premium Tax Credits if 2026 MAGI falls under 400% FPL ($63,840 single, $132,000 family of four), (2) an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan at full price for the triple tax advantage when above the subsidy cliff, or (3) a spouse's employer plan if available. ICHRA is not available to sole proprietors because the IRS does not classify an unincorporated business owner as their own employee. The Form 7206 self-employed health insurance deduction lets a sole proprietor write off 100% of premiums above the line, reducing federal income tax only, not self-employment tax on Schedule SE. For 2026, 1099 contractors and freelancers near the 400% FPL cliff can use Form 7206, HSA contributions, and retirement account contributions together to drop MAGI below the cliff and unlock thousands in subsidies.
quickAnswer.es: Los propietarios unicos, Schedule C filers y otros trabajadores independientes sin empleados suelen elegir entre (1) un plan del Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima si el MAGI 2026 esta bajo el 400% FPL ($63,840 soltero, $132,000 familia de cuatro), (2) un HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo para la ventaja triple cuando se supera el tope del subsidio, o (3) el plan del empleador del conyuge si esta disponible. El ICHRA no esta disponible para propietarios unicos porque el IRS no los clasifica como sus propios empleados. La deduccion del Formulario 7206 permite deducir el 100% de las primas, reduciendo solo el impuesto sobre la renta, no el impuesto al trabajo independiente del Schedule SE.

introParagraphs.en: Sole proprietors filing Schedule C face a coverage gap that W-2 workers never see: no employer pays half the premium, no pretax payroll deduction happens automatically, and no HR department walks you through open enrollment. A freelancer earning $70,000 a year in 1099 income is functionally their own benefits department. The upside: the IRS gives self-employed individuals and sole proprietors tax tools unavailable to wage employees, and those tools can cut effective health insurance costs by 30% or more when used correctly.
introParagraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos que declaran Schedule C enfrentan una brecha de cobertura que los empleados W-2 nunca ven: ningun empleador paga la mitad de la prima, no hay deduccion automatica antes de impuestos via nomina, y ningun departamento de RR.HH. los guia durante la inscripcion. Un freelancer que gana $70,000 al ano en ingresos 1099 es funcionalmente su propio departamento de beneficios. El lado positivo: el IRS da a los trabajadores independientes y propietarios unicos herramientas fiscales que los asalariados no tienen, y usadas correctamente pueden reducir el costo efectivo del seguro medico un 30% o mas.

introParagraphs.en: One common point of confusion for sole proprietors in 2026 is the ICHRA (Individual Coverage Health Reimbursement Arrangement). Some brokers pitch ICHRA as a route for self-employed buyers, but the IRS rule is clear: a sole proprietor is not considered an employee of their own unincorporated business, so ICHRA is not available. Only if the business is structured as a C-Corporation and the owner is a W-2 employee of that corporation does ICHRA become an option. For the vast majority of unincorporated sole proprietors, Schedule C filers, and independent contractors, the Marketplace and the Form 7206 deduction are the primary tools. This guide covers each option for 2026 with the actual numbers.
introParagraphs.es: Un punto de confusion comun para propietarios unicos en 2026 es el ICHRA (Acuerdo de Reembolso de Salud de Cobertura Individual). Algunos agentes lo presentan como una opcion para trabajadores independientes, pero la regla del IRS es clara: un propietario unico no se considera empleado de su propio negocio no incorporado, por lo que el ICHRA no esta disponible. Solo si el negocio esta estructurado como C-Corporation y el dueno es empleado W-2 de esa corporacion el ICHRA se vuelve una opcion. Para la gran mayoria de propietarios unicos no incorporados, Schedule C filers y contratistas independientes, el Mercado y la deduccion del Formulario 7206 son las herramientas principales.

en: ACA Marketplace with Premium Tax Credits
Sole proprietors with 2026 MAGI under 400% FPL ($63,840 single)
es: Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima
Propietarios unicos con MAGI 2026 bajo 400% FPL ($63,840 soltero)

en: Sole proprietors and 1099 contractors above the 400% FPL subsidy cliff
es: HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo
Propietarios unicos y contratistas 1099 por encima del tope del 400% FPL

en: Married sole proprietors with an employed spouse
es: Propietarios unicos casados con conyuge empleado
$0 a $400/mes (antes de impuestos via nomina del conyuge)

en: COBRA from a prior W-2 job
Freelancers recently self-employed after leaving a salaried position
es: COBRA de un empleo W-2 anterior
Freelancers recien independizados despues de dejar un puesto asalariado

footnote.en: ICHRA is not available to sole proprietors. Only C-Corp owner-employees are eligible. Subsidies phase down approaching 400% FPL and stop at 400%. Enhanced PTCs from the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026.
footnote.es: El ICHRA no esta disponible para propietarios unicos. Solo los duenos-empleados de C-Corp son elegibles. Los subsidios se reducen gradualmente acercandose al 400% FPL y se detienen al llegar. Los PTCs mejorados de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026.

heading.en: Option 1: ACA Marketplace with Premium Tax Credits
heading.es: Opcion 1: Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors with a projected 2026 MAGI below 400% FPL ($63,840 for a single filer, $132,000 for a household of four) qualify for Premium Tax Credits on marketplace plans. The subsidy cliff is back for 2026 after enhanced PTCs from the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026. Subsidies phase down as income climbs approaching 400% FPL and stop entirely at 400%. A self-employed individual earning $1 over $63,840 owes the full unsubsidized premium, which for a 40-year-old can easily run $600 to $900 per month on a Silver plan.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos con un MAGI 2026 proyectado bajo el 400% FPL ($63,840 para solteros, $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro) califican para Creditos Fiscales de Prima en los planes del mercado. El tope del subsidio regreso en 2026 tras la expiracion de los PTCs mejorados de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion el 1 de enero de 2026. Los subsidios se reducen gradualmente al acercarse al 400% FPL y se detienen completamente al llegar. Un trabajador independiente que gane $1 mas de $63,840 debe pagar la prima completa sin subsidio.

paragraphs.en: The MAGI calculation for sole proprietors and Schedule C filers is not the same as gross 1099 income. MAGI for ACA purposes starts with net Schedule C profit, subtracts half of self-employment tax, subtracts the Form 7206 health insurance deduction itself, and subtracts any SEP-IRA, Solo 401(k), or HSA contributions. A freelancer with $90,000 in gross 1099 income can often reach a MAGI of $55,000 to $65,000 after those deductions stack, landing just under the subsidy cliff. The marketplace reconciles estimated vs. actual MAGI at tax time via Form 1095-A, which you use to complete Form 8962 (Premium Tax Credit reconciliation). Underestimating MAGI means owing credits back at tax time.
paragraphs.es: El calculo del MAGI para propietarios unicos y Schedule C filers no es igual al ingreso bruto 1099. El MAGI para el ACA comienza con la ganancia neta del Schedule C, resta la mitad del impuesto al trabajo independiente, resta la deduccion del Formulario 7206, y resta aportes al SEP-IRA, Solo 401(k) o HSA. Un freelancer con $90,000 en ingresos brutos 1099 puede llegar a un MAGI de $55,000 a $65,000 despues de esas deducciones, quedando justo debajo del tope del subsidio. El mercado reconcilia el MAGI estimado vs. real en la declaracion de impuestos via Formulario 1095-A.

heading.en: Option 2: HSA-Qualified HDHP at Full Price
heading.es: Opcion 2: HDHP compatible con HSA a precio completo

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors above the 2026 subsidy cliff, including higher-earning independent contractors and unincorporated business owners with net income above $65,000 to $75,000 after deductions, typically find an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan the most tax-efficient option available. Without subsidies, premiums on rich Bronze or Silver plans become expensive. An HSA-qualified HDHP (2026 minimum deductible: $1,700 self / $3,400 family; 2026 contribution limit: $4,400 self / $8,750 family plus $1,000 catch-up at age 55+) has the lowest sticker premium and opens a Health Savings Account with triple tax advantages.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos por encima del tope del subsidio 2026, incluidos contratistas independientes y duenos de negocios no incorporados con ingresos netos por encima de $65,000 a $75,000 despues de deducciones, tipicamente encuentran que un HDHP compatible con HSA es la opcion mas eficiente fiscalmente disponible. Sin subsidios, las primas de planes Bronze o Silver enriquecidos se vuelven costosas. Un HDHP compatible con HSA (deducible minimo 2026: $1,700 individual / $3,400 familia; limite de aporte 2026: $4,400 individual / $8,750 familia mas $1,000 adicional a los 55+) tiene la prima mas baja y abre una Cuenta de Ahorros para Salud con triple ventaja fiscal.

paragraphs.en: The HSA triple tax advantage works as follows for a sole proprietor: contributions reduce federal income tax above the line (via Form 8889, flowing to Schedule 1 line 13), growth is tax-free inside the account, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. A 1099 contractor in the 24% federal bracket who maxes a family HSA at $8,750 in 2026 saves roughly $2,100 in federal income tax. HSA contributions also reduce MAGI, potentially pushing a Schedule C filer below the 400% FPL cliff and unlocking marketplace subsidies. Unlike a Flexible Spending Account, which is employer-only and use-it-or-lose-it, an HSA is portable and the balance rolls over indefinitely. Most self-employed individuals have no FSA access at all.
paragraphs.es: La triple ventaja fiscal del HSA funciona asi para un propietario unico: los aportes reducen el impuesto federal sobre la renta above-the-line (via Formulario 8889, al Schedule 1 linea 13), el crecimiento es libre de impuestos dentro de la cuenta, y los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos. Un contratista 1099 en el rango del 24% que maximiza un HSA familiar en $8,750 en 2026 ahorra aproximadamente $2,100 en impuesto federal. A diferencia de una Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA), que es solo para empleadores y perdible si no se usa, un HSA es portatil y el saldo pasa de ano en ano. La mayoria de trabajadores independientes no tienen acceso a FSA.

heading.es: Opcion 3: Plan del empleador del conyuge

paragraphs.en: Married sole proprietors whose spouse holds a W-2 job with health benefits can join the spouse's employer plan, often at the lowest net cost of any option. Employer plans are funded pretax through payroll, which avoids both income tax and FICA on the employee contribution. A sole proprietor on a spouse plan cannot claim the Form 7206 deduction for any month they were eligible for that employer plan, even if they chose not to enroll. Joining is only possible during the spouse's annual open enrollment or within 60 days of a qualifying life event such as marriage, birth of a child, loss of the sole proprietor's other coverage, or a permanent move outside the plan's service area.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos casados cuyo conyuge tiene empleo W-2 con beneficios de salud pueden unirse al plan del empleador del conyuge, frecuentemente con el costo neto mas bajo de todas las opciones. Los planes del empleador se financian antes de impuestos via nomina, lo que evita el impuesto sobre la renta y el FICA sobre la contribucion del empleado. Un propietario unico en el plan del conyuge no puede reclamar la deduccion del Formulario 7206 por ningun mes en que fue elegible para ese plan del empleador, incluso si eligio no inscribirse.

heading.en: Option 4: COBRA from a Prior W-2 Job
heading.es: Opcion 4: COBRA de un empleo W-2 anterior

paragraphs.en: Newly self-employed freelancers and independent contractors who recently left salaried employment can elect COBRA continuation coverage for up to 18 months. Under COBRA, the sole proprietor pays both the employee and employer shares of the premium plus a 2% administrative fee, typically turning a $200/month payroll contribution into a $1,000 to $1,800/month bill. COBRA is worth keeping only if you are mid-treatment with in-network specialists who are not on any marketplace plan. Leaving a W-2 job is itself a qualifying life event triggering a 60-day Marketplace Special Enrollment Period. Most unincorporated business owners drop COBRA within the first month and enroll in a marketplace plan with the SE deduction applied, which cuts the effective cost significantly.
paragraphs.es: Los freelancers y contratistas independientes recien independizados que dejaron un empleo asalariado pueden elegir la continuacion de cobertura COBRA por hasta 18 meses. Con COBRA, el propietario unico paga las partes del empleado y del empleador de la prima mas una tarifa administrativa del 2%, convirtiendo tipicamente una contribucion de $200/mes en una factura de $1,000 a $1,800/mes. COBRA solo vale la pena si esta en medio de un tratamiento con especialistas dentro de la red que no estan en ningun plan del mercado. Dejar un empleo W-2 es en si un evento calificador que activa un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial de 60 dias en el Mercado.

intro.en: Sole proprietors are among the most actively marketed segments for non-ACA coverage products. These are the options that look attractive and carry serious financial risk:
intro.es: Los propietarios unicos son uno de los segmentos mas comercializados para productos de cobertura que no son ACA. Estas son las opciones que parecen atractivas pero conllevan serios riesgos financieros:

en: ICHRA is only available to employees. A sole proprietor (unincorporated business owner) is not considered an employee of their own business under IRS rules. Any broker pitching ICHRA to a sole proprietor is either confused about the rules or selling a product that will fail at claims time.
es: El ICHRA solo esta disponible para empleados. Un propietario unico (negocio no incorporado) no se considera empleado de su propio negocio bajo las reglas del IRS. Cualquier agente que ofrezca ICHRA a un propietario unico esta confundido o vendiendo un producto que fallara cuando haga reclamaciones.

en: Do not have to cover pre-existing conditions, can rescind coverage retroactively, and do not count as minimum essential coverage under the ACA. A sole proprietor with a single hospitalization can face a six-figure bill with no ACA protections.
es: Planes a corto plazo de duracion limitada

No tienen que cubrir condiciones preexistentes, pueden cancelar la cobertura retroactivamente y no cuentan como cobertura esencial minima bajo el ACA. Un propietario unico con una hospitalizacion puede enfrentar una factura de seis cifras sin protecciones del ACA.

en: Health share ministries (Medi-Share, Liberty HealthShare, Samaritan)

NOT insurance. No legal obligation to pay claims. Pre-existing conditions are typically excluded. Lifestyle clauses covering tobacco use, alcohol, mental health, and reproductive care can disqualify entire treatment categories.
es: Ministerios de compartir salud (Medi-Share, Liberty HealthShare, Samaritan)

NO son seguro. No hay obligacion legal de pagar reclamaciones. Las condiciones preexistentes tipicamente se excluyen. Las clausulas de estilo de vida sobre tabaco, alcohol, salud mental y atencion reproductiva pueden descalificar categorias enteras de tratamiento.

en: Claiming the Form 7206 deduction for months eligible for a spouse's plan

The self-employed health insurance deduction is disallowed for any month you or your spouse were eligible for an employer-sponsored plan, even if you did not enroll. This is one of the most audited areas on Schedule C returns. The IRS uses Form W-2 data from the spouse to flag eligibility.
es: Reclamar la deduccion del Formulario 7206 en meses elegibles para el plan del conyuge

La deduccion de seguro medico para autonomos no esta permitida por ningun mes en que usted o su conyuge fueron elegibles para un plan del empleador, incluso si no se inscribieron. Esta es una de las areas mas auditadas en las declaraciones Schedule C.

en: Misjudging the 400% FPL subsidy cliff in 2026

Earning $1 over 400% FPL ($63,840 single in 2026) can cost $5,000 to $15,000 in lost Premium Tax Credits. Self-employed individuals and 1099 contractors near the cliff should time HSA contributions, retirement account deposits, and the Form 7206 deduction to land MAGI just under the threshold.
es: Calcular mal el tope del subsidio del 400% FPL en 2026

Ganar $1 por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840 soltero en 2026) puede costar $5,000 a $15,000 en creditos fiscales de prima perdidos. Los trabajadores independientes y contratistas 1099 cerca del tope deben programar aportes al HSA, al retiro y la deduccion del Formulario 7206 para que el MAGI quede justo por debajo del umbral.

footnote.en: Verify any plan covers all 10 ACA essential health benefits and is sold on healthcare.gov or your state's exchange. Plans sold off-exchange by associations often exclude essential benefits.
footnote.es: Verifique que cualquier plan cubra los 10 beneficios esenciales del ACA y se venda en healthcare.gov o el intercambio de su estado. Los planes vendidos fuera del intercambio por asociaciones frecuentemente excluyen beneficios esenciales.

heading.en: Self-Employment Health Insurance Deduction (Form 7206) for Sole Proprietors
heading.es: Deduccion de Seguro Medico para Trabajadores Independientes (Formulario 7206) para Propietarios Unicos

paragraphs.en: Form 7206 lets a sole proprietor, Schedule C filer, or unincorporated business owner write off 100% of health insurance premiums paid for themselves, their spouse, and their dependents as an above-the-line adjustment on Schedule 1, line 17. The deduction flows from Form 7206 through Schedule 1 to Form 1040, reducing adjusted gross income and MAGI. This means the deduction raises next year's ACA subsidy eligibility indirectly by lowering MAGI. A sole proprietor paying $800 per month in premiums ($9,600 per year) in the 22% federal bracket saves roughly $2,112 in federal income tax via Form 7206.
paragraphs.es: El Formulario 7206 permite a un propietario unico, Schedule C filer o dueno de negocio no incorporado deducir el 100% de las primas de seguro medico pagadas por si mismo, su conyuge y dependientes como un ajuste above-the-line en el Schedule 1, linea 17. La deduccion fluye del Formulario 7206 al Schedule 1 y al Form 1040, reduciendo el ingreso bruto ajustado y el MAGI. Esto significa que la deduccion aumenta indirectamente la elegibilidad para subsidios del ACA del proximo ano al bajar el MAGI.

paragraphs.en: Critical caveat: Form 7206 reduces income tax only. The deduction does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. Self-employment tax is 15.3% (12.4% Social Security plus 2.9% Medicare) and is calculated on net SE earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. The Form 7206 deduction sits on Schedule 1 and does not feed into Schedule SE at any point. Claiming it reduces SE tax is a factual error that will produce incorrect returns and potential IRS notices. The only SE tax break available to sole proprietors is the deduction of one-half of SE tax itself (also on Schedule 1), not the health insurance deduction.
paragraphs.es: Advertencia critica: el Formulario 7206 reduce SOLO el impuesto sobre la renta. La deduccion NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente del Schedule SE. El impuesto SE es del 15.3% (12.4% del Seguro Social mas 2.9% de Medicare) y se calcula sobre las ganancias netas SE antes de aplicar la deduccion del seguro medico. Reclamar que reduce el impuesto SE es un error factual que producira declaraciones incorrectas y posibles avisos del IRS.

paragraphs.en: Two additional limits apply to independent contractors and Schedule C filers using Form 7206. First, the deduction cannot exceed net self-employment earnings minus one-half of self-employment tax. Second, any month you or your spouse were eligible for an employer-sponsored plan disqualifies that month's premiums from the deduction, even if you did not enroll. Long-term care insurance premiums also qualify under Form 7206 within age-based limits ($500 at age 40 or under, $930 at 41 to 50, $1,860 at 51 to 60, $4,960 at 61 to 70, $6,200 over 70 in 2026).
paragraphs.es: Dos limites adicionales aplican a contratistas independientes y Schedule C filers que usan el Formulario 7206. Primero, la deduccion no puede exceder las ganancias netas del trabajo independiente menos la mitad del impuesto SE. Segundo, cualquier mes en que usted o su conyuge fueran elegibles para un plan del empleador descalifica las primas de ese mes de la deduccion, incluso si no se inscribieron. Las primas de seguro de cuidado a largo plazo tambien califican bajo el Formulario 7206 dentro de limites por edad.

heading.en: Premium Tax Credit (PTC) Eligibility for Sole Proprietors in 2026
heading.es: Elegibilidad para el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) para Propietarios Unicos en 2026

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors projecting their 2026 MAGI need one benchmark number: 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. In 2026 that is $63,840 for a single filer and $132,000 for a household of four. Below that line, the Premium Tax Credit (PTC) phases down as income climbs. Subsidies do not snap off at 250% or 300% FPL. At 400% FPL they stop entirely, and above 400% FPL a self-employed individual pays the full unsubsidized marketplace premium. For 2026, the income threshold structure by household size is as follows: 138% FPL (Medicaid expansion threshold in most states) through 400% FPL (PTC cliff).
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos que proyectan su MAGI 2026 necesitan un numero de referencia: 400% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza. En 2026 eso es $63,840 para un declarante individual y $132,000 para un hogar de cuatro. Por debajo de esa linea, el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) se reduce gradualmente a medida que el ingreso sube. Los subsidios no se cortan abruptamente al 250% o 300% FPL. Al 400% FPL se detienen completamente, y por encima del 400% FPL un trabajador independiente paga la prima completa sin subsidio.

paragraphs.en: For a Schedule C filer or sole proprietor, MAGI for ACA PTC purposes is calculated from net self-employment income (not gross 1099 receipts), minus half of self-employment tax, minus the Form 7206 premium deduction, minus any HSA contributions, minus retirement account contributions to a SEP-IRA or Solo 401(k), plus any tax-exempt interest or tax-exempt Social Security benefits. At tax time, marketplace enrollees receive Form 1095-A from their exchange, which is used to complete Form 8962 reconciling advance PTCs received with actual PTC eligibility based on final MAGI. Underestimating MAGI creates a repayment obligation; overestimating results in a refundable credit.
paragraphs.es: Para un Schedule C filer o propietario unico, el MAGI para propositos del PTC del ACA se calcula a partir del ingreso neto del trabajo independiente (no ingresos brutos 1099), menos la mitad del impuesto SE, menos la deduccion del Formulario 7206, menos aportes al HSA, menos aportes al SEP-IRA o Solo 401(k), mas intereses exentos de impuestos o beneficios del Seguro Social exentos. En la declaracion de impuestos, los inscritos en el mercado reciben el Formulario 1095-A, que se usa para completar el Formulario 8962.

caption.en: 2026 ACA subsidy income thresholds by household size (48 contiguous states + DC)
caption.es: Umbrales de ingreso para subsidios ACA 2026 por tamano de hogar (48 estados contiguos + DC)

footnote.en: 138% FPL figures derived from 2026 HHS guidelines ($15,960 base for household of 1, +$5,680 per additional person). 400% FPL = 4x the base FPL. Alaska and Hawaii have higher thresholds. Medicaid expansion operates in 40 states plus DC as of 2026. Source: HHS ASPE 2026 Poverty Guidelines.
footnote.es: Las cifras del 138% FPL se derivan de las pautas HHS 2026 ($15,960 base para hogar de 1, +$5,680 por persona adicional). 400% FPL = 4x el FPL base. Alaska y Hawaii tienen umbrales mas altos. La expansion de Medicaid opera en 40 estados mas DC a partir de 2026.

heading.en: HSA and HDHP Fit for Sole Proprietors in 2026
heading.es: Compatibilidad HSA y HDHP para Propietarios Unicos en 2026

paragraphs.en: A sole proprietor can open and fund a Health Savings Account for any year in which they are enrolled in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan for the full year. The 2026 HDHP minimum deductible is $1,700 for self-only coverage and $3,400 for family coverage (per IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19). The 2026 HSA contribution limit is $4,400 for self-only and $8,750 for family, with an additional $1,000 catch-up contribution allowed at age 55 and older. The HSA triple tax advantage applies: contributions are tax-deductible above the line (reducing MAGI), earnings grow tax-free, and qualified medical expense withdrawals are tax-free.
paragraphs.es: Un propietario unico puede abrir y financiar una Cuenta de Ahorros para Salud (HSA) en cualquier ano en que este inscrito en un HDHP compatible con HSA durante todo el ano. El deducible minimo del HDHP 2026 es $1,700 para cobertura individual y $3,400 para familia (segun el Rev. Proc. 2025-19 del IRS). El limite de aporte al HSA 2026 es $4,400 individual y $8,750 familiar, con un aporte adicional de $1,000 permitido a los 55 anos o mas. La triple ventaja fiscal del HSA aplica: las contribuciones son deducibles above-the-line (reduciendo el MAGI), las ganancias crecen libres de impuestos, y los retiros por gastos medicos calificados son libres de impuestos.

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors and freelancers should distinguish HSA from FSA clearly. A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is an employer-administered benefit available only through an employer's benefit plan. An unincorporated business owner or 1099 contractor with no employer has no FSA access. The HSA is self-directed and not tied to any employer, making it the only tax-advantaged medical savings tool available to sole proprietors. Not every HDHP sold on the marketplace is HSA-qualified. The 2026 ACA Marketplace out-of-pocket maximum is $10,600 for individual coverage and $21,200 for family coverage, which is higher than the HDHP maximum out-of-pocket cap of $8,500 self / $17,000 family. A plan with an OOP maximum above the HDHP cap does not qualify for HSA pairing, so check the plan's HSA-eligible label explicitly before purchasing.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos y freelancers deben distinguir claramente el HSA del FSA. Una Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) es un beneficio administrado por el empleador disponible solo a traves del plan de beneficios de un empleador. Un dueno de negocio no incorporado o contratista 1099 sin empleador no tiene acceso a FSA. El HSA es autodirigido y no esta vinculado a ningun empleador, haciendolo la unica herramienta de ahorro medico con ventajas fiscales disponible para propietarios unicos. No todos los HDHP vendidos en el mercado son compatibles con HSA.

caption.en: 2026 HSA and HDHP limits for sole proprietors (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19)
caption.es: Limites HSA y HDHP 2026 para propietarios unicos (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19)

en: ACA Marketplace OOP max (separate from HDHP cap)
es: Maximo de bolsillo del mercado ACA (separado del tope HDHP)

footnote.en: Not every marketplace HDHP is HSA-qualified. The ACA OOP max ($10,600 individual) is higher than the HDHP OOP cap ($8,500), so a plan with OOP between $8,500 and $10,600 does not qualify for HSA pairing. Always confirm HSA-eligibility in the plan label.
footnote.es: No todos los HDHP del mercado son compatibles con HSA. El maximo de bolsillo ACA ($10,600 individual) es mas alto que el tope HDHP ($8,500), por lo que un plan con OOP entre $8,500 y $10,600 no califica para HSA. Siempre confirme la elegibilidad HSA en la etiqueta del plan.

heading.en: Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) Triggers for Sole Proprietors
heading.es: Factores Desencadenantes del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado para Propietarios Unicos

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors who miss the annual Marketplace Open Enrollment window (November 1 through January 15 for most states) can still enroll in a marketplace plan within a 60-day Special Enrollment Period triggered by a qualifying life event. Self-employed freelancers and independent contractors typically encounter SEP opportunities during major business and life transitions. The SEP window is generally 60 days from the qualifying event date, though some state-based marketplaces offer 90 days.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos que pierden la ventana de Inscripcion Abierta del Mercado (1 de noviembre al 15 de enero para la mayoria de estados) aun pueden inscribirse en un plan del mercado dentro de un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial de 60 dias activado por un evento calificador de vida. Los freelancers y contratistas independientes tipicamente encuentran oportunidades SEP durante transiciones importantes de negocios y de vida.

paragraphs.en: Qualifying events for sole proprietors include: (1) loss of other minimum essential coverage such as COBRA expiring, spouse losing employer coverage, or aging off a parent's plan at 26; (2) starting a business and therefore losing access to employer-sponsored coverage; (3) marriage or divorce (60 days from the date); (4) birth, adoption, or placement of a foster child; (5) a permanent move to a new state or county that changes available marketplace plans; (6) a significant income change that causes a move from Medicaid eligibility into the marketplace subsidy range or vice versa; and (7) release from incarceration. For sole proprietors projecting income changes mid-year, updating the marketplace within 30 days of any significant income change can raise advance premium credits immediately without waiting for a full SEP trigger.
paragraphs.es: Los eventos calificadores para propietarios unicos incluyen: (1) perdida de otra cobertura esencial minima como vencimiento de COBRA, el conyuge pierde cobertura del empleador, o cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres; (2) comenzar un negocio y por lo tanto perder acceso a la cobertura del empleador; (3) matrimonio o divorcio (60 dias desde la fecha); (4) nacimiento, adopcion o colocacion de un nino de crianza; (5) mudanza permanente a un nuevo estado o condado que cambia los planes del mercado disponibles; (6) cambio significativo de ingresos que causa un movimiento entre la elegibilidad para Medicaid y el rango de subsidios del mercado; y (7) liberacion de prision.

list.en: Losing minimum essential coverage (COBRA expiration, spouse job loss): 60-day SEP window
list.es: Perder cobertura esencial minima (vencimiento COBRA, perdida de trabajo del conyuge): ventana SEP de 60 dias

list.en: Becoming self-employed after leaving W-2 employment and losing employer-sponsored plan: 60-day SEP
list.es: Convertirse en autoutonomo despues de dejar empleo W-2 y perder el plan del empleador: SEP de 60 dias
Matrimonio o asociacion domestica: SEP de 60 dias

list.en: Birth, adoption, or foster child placement: 60-day SEP (coverage can be retroactive to birth date)
list.es: Nacimiento, adopcion o colocacion de hijo de crianza: SEP de 60 dias (cobertura puede ser retroactiva a la fecha de nacimiento)

list.en: Permanent move to new state or county with different plan options: 60-day SEP
list.es: Mudanza permanente a nuevo estado o condado con diferentes opciones de plan: SEP de 60 dias

list.en: Income change moving from Medicaid eligibility to marketplace range: immediate SEP (some states 60 days)
list.es: Cambio de ingreso que mueve de elegibilidad Medicaid al rango del mercado: SEP inmediato (algunos estados 60 dias)

list.en: Aging off a parent's plan at 26: 60-day SEP
list.es: Cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres: SEP de 60 dias

heading.en: How Sole Proprietors Enroll in a Marketplace Plan in 2026
heading.es: Como se Inscriben los Propietarios Unicos en un Plan del Mercado en 2026

paragraphs.en: Sole proprietors, independent contractors, and unincorporated business owners enroll in marketplace health insurance through healthcare.gov (for states using the federal exchange) or their state's own exchange portal (California's Covered California, New York's NY State of Health, and others). Open Enrollment for 2026 plan-year coverage runs November 1 through January 15 in most states; coverage starting January 1 requires enrollment by December 15. Outside Open Enrollment, a qualifying life event triggers a 60-day Special Enrollment Period.
paragraphs.es: Los propietarios unicos, contratistas independientes y duenos de negocios no incorporados se inscriben en el seguro medico del mercado a traves de healthcare.gov (para estados que usan el intercambio federal) o el portal propio del estado (Covered California en California, NY State of Health en Nueva York, y otros).

list.en: Step 1: Gather documents needed: prior year's Schedule C (or income estimate), Social Security numbers for all family members, immigration documents if applicable, employer coverage information for any months you had employer access.
list.es: Paso 1: Reunir documentos necesarios: Schedule C del ano anterior (o estimado de ingresos), numeros del Seguro Social de todos los miembros del hogar, documentos de inmigracion si aplica, informacion de cobertura del empleador para los meses con acceso al empleador.

list.en: Step 2: Visit healthcare.gov or your state exchange and create an account. Estimate your 2026 MAGI carefully using projected net SE income minus half SE tax minus Form 7206 deduction minus any HSA and retirement contributions.
list.es: Paso 2: Visitar healthcare.gov o el intercambio de su estado y crear una cuenta. Estimar el MAGI 2026 con cuidado usando el ingreso neto SE proyectado menos la mitad del impuesto SE menos la deduccion del Formulario 7206 menos aportes al HSA y al retiro.

list.en: Step 3: Compare Silver, Bronze, and Gold plans. If MAGI is below 250% FPL ($39,900 single in 2026), Silver plans come with cost-sharing reductions that dramatically lower deductibles and copays. If above 400% FPL, compare Bronze HDHPs for HSA eligibility.
list.es: Paso 3: Comparar planes Silver, Bronze y Gold. Si el MAGI esta bajo el 250% FPL ($39,900 soltero en 2026), los planes Silver tienen reducciones de costos compartidos que bajan dramaticamente deducibles y copagos. Si esta por encima del 400% FPL, comparar HDHP Bronze para elegibilidad HSA.

list.en: Step 4: Enroll and confirm your coverage start date. Pay the first premium by the due date or coverage will not activate.
list.es: Paso 4: Inscribirse y confirmar la fecha de inicio de cobertura. Pagar la primera prima antes de la fecha limite o la cobertura no se activara.

list.en: Step 5: Update your marketplace account within 30 days of any significant income change during the year. A new large contract, a lost client, or a life event can shift your advance credit amount.
list.es: Paso 5: Actualizar la cuenta del mercado dentro de los 30 dias de cualquier cambio significativo de ingreso durante el ano. Un nuevo contrato grande, un cliente perdido o un evento de vida puede cambiar su cantidad de credito anticipado.

en.question: What is the cheapest health insurance for a sole proprietor in 2026?
en.answer: If your 2026 MAGI falls under 400% FPL ($63,840 for a single filer), an ACA marketplace Silver or Bronze plan with Premium Tax Credits is typically the most affordable option. Subsidies can reduce premiums to $50 to $300 per month depending on income and age. If you are above 400% FPL without subsidies, an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP paired with a maxed Health Savings Account usually wins on after-tax cost. The Form 7206 deduction reduces the effective cost of any marketplace plan by your marginal income tax rate. Catastrophic plans on the marketplace are restricted to people under age 30 or those with a hardship exemption. Sole proprietors aged 30 and older are not eligible for catastrophic plans regardless of income.

en.question: Do sole proprietors qualify for the Premium Tax Credit in 2026?
en.answer: Yes, if their projected 2026 MAGI falls between 100% FPL ($15,960 for a single person) and 400% FPL ($63,840 for a single person). A sole proprietor's MAGI for ACA purposes is net Schedule C income minus half of self-employment tax, minus the Form 7206 deduction, minus HSA contributions, minus retirement account contributions. The subsidy cliff is back for 2026 at 400% FPL. At tax time, Form 1095-A from the marketplace is used to reconcile advance credits on Form 8962 (the PTC form).

en.question: Can a sole proprietor deduct health insurance premiums on taxes?
en.answer: Yes. A sole proprietor with net self-employment income can deduct 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums for themselves, their spouse, and their dependents as an above-the-line adjustment using Form 7206 (Schedule 1, line 17). This deduction reduces federal income tax and MAGI but does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. The 15.3% SE tax is calculated on net earnings before the health insurance deduction is applied. Do not claim the deduction for any month you or your spouse were eligible for an employer-sponsored plan.

en.question: Can a sole proprietor use an HSA?
en.answer: Yes, as long as the sole proprietor is enrolled in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan. The 2026 HDHP minimum deductible is $1,700 for self-only and $3,400 for family coverage. The 2026 HSA contribution limit is $4,400 self and $8,750 family, plus $1,000 catch-up at age 55+. HSA contributions are deductible above the line, grow tax-free, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free (the triple tax advantage). A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is not available to sole proprietors without employees, since FSA requires an employer plan to administer it.

en.question: Is ICHRA available to sole proprietors?
en.answer: No. An Individual Coverage Health Reimbursement Arrangement (ICHRA) can only be used by employers to reimburse employees for individual health insurance. A sole proprietor is not considered an employee of their own unincorporated business under IRS rules, so ICHRA is not available. The only way a sole proprietor can access ICHRA benefits is if the business is restructured as a C-Corporation and the owner becomes a W-2 employee of that corporation. Any broker offering ICHRA to a sole proprietor operating as a Schedule C filer or single-member LLC taxed as a sole proprietorship should be viewed with skepticism.

en.question: What if a sole proprietor earns too much for ACA subsidies in 2026?
en.answer: Above 400% FPL ($63,840 single in 2026), Premium Tax Credits stop entirely. The best strategy for self-employed individuals and 1099 contractors above the cliff: (1) maximize HSA contributions ($4,400 self / $8,750 family), which reduce MAGI; (2) maximize retirement contributions to a SEP-IRA (up to 25% of net compensation) or Solo 401(k); (3) apply the Form 7206 deduction; and (4) time large deductible expenses to land MAGI just below $63,840 if close. If truly above the cliff, an HSA-qualified Bronze HDHP at full price is usually the most tax-efficient plan.

en.question: When can a sole proprietor enroll in a marketplace plan outside of Open Enrollment?
en.answer: A sole proprietor can enroll outside Open Enrollment during a 60-day Marketplace Special Enrollment Period triggered by a qualifying life event. Common SEP triggers include: losing other minimum essential coverage (COBRA expiring, spouse losing employer coverage), becoming self-employed after leaving a W-2 job, marriage or divorce, birth or adoption of a child, moving to a new state or county, or a significant income change. Open Enrollment for 2026 coverage runs November 1 through January 15. The low-income SEP (year-round enrollment below 150% FPL) was eliminated for 2026.

en.question: Can a sole proprietor stay on a parent's plan?
en.answer: Yes, if under age 26. Under ACA Section 2714, children can remain on a parent's health plan until their 26th birthday regardless of student status, marital status, employment, or financial dependence. A self-employed sole proprietor under 26 can remain on a parent's ACA or employer plan. The Form 7206 deduction is not available for months covered under a parent's plan, since the sole proprietor paid no premiums. After turning 26, the loss of parental coverage triggers a 60-day Special Enrollment Period on the marketplace.

es.question: ?Cual es el seguro medico mas barato para un propietario unico en 2026?
es.answer: Si su MAGI 2026 es menor al 400% FPL ($63,840 para un declarante individual), un plan Silver o Bronze del mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima es tipicamente la opcion mas asequible. Los subsidios pueden reducir las primas a $50 a $300 por mes segun el ingreso y la edad. Si supera el 400% FPL sin subsidios, un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA combinado con un HSA maximizado generalmente gana en costo despues de impuestos. La deduccion del Formulario 7206 reduce el costo efectivo de cualquier plan del mercado en su tasa impositiva marginal. Los planes catastroficos en el mercado estan restringidos a personas menores de 30 anos o con una exencion por dificultades economicas. Los propietarios unicos de 30 anos o mas no son elegibles para planes catastroficos.

es.question: ?Los propietarios unicos califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima en 2026?
es.answer: Si, si su MAGI 2026 proyectado esta entre el 100% FPL ($15,960 para una persona) y el 400% FPL ($63,840 para una persona). El MAGI de un propietario unico para propositos del ACA es el ingreso neto del Schedule C menos la mitad del impuesto SE, menos la deduccion del Formulario 7206, menos aportes al HSA, menos aportes a cuentas de retiro. El tope del subsidio regreso al 400% FPL en 2026. En la declaracion de impuestos, el Formulario 1095-A del mercado se usa para reconciliar los creditos anticipados en el Formulario 8962.

es.question: ?Puede un propietario unico deducir las primas de seguro medico en los impuestos?
es.answer: Si. Un propietario unico con ingresos netos del trabajo independiente puede deducir el 100% de las primas medicas, dentales y de vision para si mismo, su conyuge y dependientes como ajuste above-the-line usando el Formulario 7206 (Schedule 1, linea 17). Esta deduccion reduce el impuesto federal sobre la renta y el MAGI, pero NO reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente del Schedule SE. El 15.3% del impuesto SE se calcula sobre las ganancias netas antes de aplicar la deduccion del seguro medico.

es.question: ?Puede un propietario unico usar un HSA?
es.answer: Si, siempre que el propietario unico este inscrito en un HDHP compatible con HSA. El deducible minimo del HDHP 2026 es $1,700 individual y $3,400 familiar. El limite de aporte al HSA 2026 es $4,400 individual y $8,750 familiar, mas $1,000 adicional a los 55+. Los aportes al HSA son deducibles above-the-line, el crecimiento es libre de impuestos, y los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos (la triple ventaja fiscal). Una FSA no esta disponible para propietarios unicos sin empleados, ya que la FSA requiere un plan del empleador para administrarla.

es.question: ?Esta disponible el ICHRA para propietarios unicos?
es.answer: No. Un ICHRA solo puede ser usado por empleadores para reembolsar a empleados por seguro medico individual. Un propietario unico no se considera empleado de su propio negocio no incorporado bajo las reglas del IRS, por lo que el ICHRA no esta disponible. La unica forma en que un propietario unico puede acceder al ICHRA es si el negocio se reestructura como C-Corporation y el dueno se convierte en empleado W-2 de esa corporacion.

es.question: ?Que pasa si un propietario unico gana demasiado para los subsidios del ACA en 2026?
es.answer: Por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840 soltero en 2026), los Creditos Fiscales de Prima se detienen completamente. La mejor estrategia para trabajadores independientes y contratistas 1099 por encima del tope: (1) maximizar aportes al HSA ($4,400 individual / $8,750 familiar), que reducen el MAGI; (2) maximizar aportes de retiro a un SEP-IRA o Solo 401(k); (3) aplicar la deduccion del Formulario 7206; y (4) programar gastos deducibles para que el MAGI quede justo debajo de $63,840 si esta cerca. Si realmente supera el tope, un HDHP Bronze compatible con HSA a precio completo es usualmente el plan mas eficiente fiscalmente.

es.question: ?Cuando puede un propietario unico inscribirse en un plan del mercado fuera de la Inscripcion Abierta?
es.answer: Un propietario unico puede inscribirse fuera de la Inscripcion Abierta durante un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial de 60 dias activado por un evento calificador. Los factores desencadenantes comunes incluyen: perder otra cobertura esencial minima, convertirse en autoempleado despues de dejar un empleo W-2, matrimonio o divorcio, nacimiento o adopcion de un nino, mudanza a un nuevo estado o condado, o un cambio significativo de ingresos. La Inscripcion Abierta para cobertura 2026 va del 1 de noviembre al 15 de enero.

es.question: ?Puede un propietario unico quedarse en el plan de los padres?
es.answer: Si, si tiene menos de 26 anos. Bajo la Seccion 2714 del ACA, los hijos pueden permanecer en el plan de salud de sus padres hasta su 26 cumpleanos independientemente del estatus de estudiante, estado civil, empleo o dependencia financiera. Un propietario unico autoempleado menor de 26 puede permanecer en el plan ACA o del empleador de sus padres. La deduccion del Formulario 7206 no esta disponible para los meses cubiertos por el plan de los padres, ya que el propietario unico no pago primas. Despues de cumplir 26 anos, la perdida de la cobertura de los padres activa un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial de 60 dias.

note.en: Official IRS revenue procedure setting the 2026 HSA contribution limits ($4,400 self / $8,750 family) and HDHP minimum deductible ($1,700 self / $3,400 family).
note.es: Procedimiento de ingresos oficial del IRS que establece los limites de aportacion al HSA 2026 ($4,400 individual / $8,750 familia) y el deducible minimo del HDHP ($1,700 individual / $3,400 familia).
